Lots of companies are in the "encourage AI adoption" phase, whether teaching them ChatGPT/Claude or (sigh) tokenmaxxing.

That dodges the harder problems of firm leadership: What do you want people to use AI for? What work should be reserved for people? What else needs to change?

"Just use AI" can be useful (I call it The Crowd in my informal framework I gave in my post below), but you also need a Lab where people can go with their ideas and Leadership to make decisions about how to incentivize people and what to do: https://t.co/bHvjoVo8Jb

今天 AI 已经让越来越多人有能力做出漂亮、有用、有想象力的东西。

但真正的鸿沟，不再只是“谁能做出来”，而是“谁能被看见”。

所以我们要把 Open Design 变成一个舞台。

只要你用它做了 plugin、skill、template、workflow，或者任何值得被看见的实验，发给我们。
